Title: MPK6-mediated HY5 phosphorylation regulates light-induced anthocyanin accumulation in apple fruit

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds MdHY5 and is associated with reduced MdHY5 stability, thereby opposing anthocyanin accumulation indirectly
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCOP1 directly interacts with MdHY5 in vitro. 2. Phosphomimic MdHY5S36D shows lower affinity for MdCOP1, whereas phosphodeficient MdHY5S36A shows higher affinity. 3. Reduced MdCOP1 binding is associated with greater MdHY5 stability and enhanced anthocyanin accumulation under light-regulated conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GST pull-down assay; MG132 proteasome inhibition assay; immunoblotting
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing MdCOP1-mediated degradation of MdHY5 could help stabilize anthocyanin-promoting regulators for improved fruit coloration
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdCOP1 was not itself genetically manipulated as the main focus, but was experimentally validated as part of the MdMPK6-MdHY5 stability mechanism.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No stable complex established; interacts with MdMPK6 and MdCOP1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation and likely stress-protective antioxidant role
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes light-induced anthocyanin accumulation; phosphorylation at Ser-36 enhances activity and stability
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHY5 is rapidly phosphorylated after light exposure and positively regulates anthocyanin accumulation in apple fruit and calli. 2. MdHY5 directly activates anthocyanin-related targets MdMYB1, MdCHI, and MdUFGT. 3. Ser-36 phosphorylation enhances MdHY5 stability and transcriptional activity, while the S36A form is impaired in promoting anthocyanin accumulation. 4. MdHY5 function in anthocyanin accumulation is dependent on MdMPK6 activity.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light; MdMPK6-mediated phosphorylation; darkness via MdCOP1-associated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; phosphorylation-dependent stabilization; reduced COP1 binding after phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by light; diurnal expression with higher transcript abundance during daytime and peak at 3 or 6 pm; higher accumulation at fruit ripening stage
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely positive autoregulation inferred from discussion, where HY5 may promote its own transcription
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light treatment; dark treatment; MG132; K252a; OA; fruit ripening stage
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis transcriptional regulation and regulator stability
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central light-responsive regulator required for maximum light-induced anthocyanin accumulation in apple fruit
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and silencing in fruit; stable overexpression and RNAi in calli; phosphomimic/phosphodeficient mutagenesis; RT-qPCR; HPLC anthocyanin profiling; in vitro kinase assay; LC-MS/MS phosphosite mapping; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H mentioned in discussion; immunoblotting
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Label-free phosphoproteomics by LC-MS/MS; phosphopeptide datasets in Tables S1-S4, accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving apple peel coloration by enhancing HY5 abundance or mimicking activating phosphorylation at Ser-36
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser-36 is the predominant phosphorylation site. Phosphomimic MdHY5S36D shows strongest anthocyanin-promoting effect; MdHY5S36A is defective.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation and likely antioxidant-related fruit quality improvement
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes light-induced anthocyanin accumulation by phosphorylating and stabilizing MdHY5
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMPK6-15G directly interacts with MdHY5 in yeast, in vitro, and in vivo. 2. MdMPK6-15G phosphorylates MdHY5 at Ser-36 and increases MdHY5 stability and target-gene activation. 3. Overexpression or constitutive activation of MdMPK6-15G increases anthocyanin accumulation, whereas silencing reduces it. 4. MdMPK6-15G acts upstream of MdHY5 and is required for maximum MdHY5 activity in light-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light; kinase/phosphatase balance affected by K252a and OA
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein phosphorylation activation and stabilization of MdHY5
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated by light; function prominent in fruit ripening stage under light
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly promotes expression of MdMYB1, MdCHI, and MdUFGT via MdHY5
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light treatment; K252a; OA; fruit ripening stage; dark condition for stability assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal transduction controlling anthocyanin biosynthesis regulator activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key upstream kinase required for full light-induced anthocyanin accumulation through MdHY5 phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; GST pull-down; Co-IP; BiFC; BLI; transient overexpression and silencing in fruit; stable transformation in calli; in vitro kinase assay; HPLC anthocyanin profiling; immunoblotting
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Label-free phosphoproteomics by LC-MS/MS; phosphoproteome tables S1-S4, accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering improved peel coloration through enhanced kinase activity or constitutively active alleles
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among the two apple MPK6 paralogs, MdMPK6-15G had higher affinity for MdHY5 and was the only tested paralog that phosphorylated MdHY5 in vitro.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds MdHY5 but was not shown to phosphorylate MdHY5 or directly promote anthocyanin accumulation in the reported assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMPK6-02G interacts with MdHY5 in Y2H, pull-down, Co-IP, BiFC, and BLI assays. 2. Its binding affinity to MdHY5 is lower than that of MdMPK6-15G. 3. In vitro kinase assay did not support MdHY5 as an MdMPK6-02G substrate.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction without demonstrated phosphorylation of MdHY5 in this study
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established as decisive for anthocyanin accumulation in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; GST pull-down; Co-IP; BiFC; BLI; in vitro kinase assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower immediate application value than MdMPK6-15G because catalytic contribution to MdHY5 activation was not supported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested as a candidate upstream kinase, but evidence indicates functional divergence from MdMPK6-15G.
